raw_consumer_statement:
I have made purchase of a tool for {$20.00} at XXXX on XX/XX/year> to be shipped to me. XXXX fraudulently billed my CitiXXXX  credit card company as a in-person credit cash advance. The payment was processed by XXXX which billed my credit card citi.com. The charge at Citi shows as in-person cash advance and Citi billed me a {$20.00} cash advance free plus high interest rate from the purchase date onward without typical 30 days grace period for customers not carrying balance. 

I have talked to all XXXX participants and explained to all of them that I never stepped foot into XXXX physical office to request a cash advance but purchased a product online to be shipped to me. 
The results : XXXX denied billing this online purchase as cash advance, even though they did the same a couple of years back when they billed my other online purchase to a different credit card company ( XXXX  ) which also charged me a cash advance fee. 
Citi refused to listen that it was an online purchase and refuse to to cancel the {$20.00} cash advance fee and interest on the transaction. 
XXXX promised to credit the {$20.00} cash advance fee to may account and start monitoring it if XXXX bills it as a cash advances again in the future. 
Since the issue happened the second time with the same XXXX online merchant but XXXX different credit card companies, I am convinced that XXXX indeed bills some purchases as cash advances, evidently in an attempt to circumvent paying a small, around 4 %, fee for credit card translations which I believe is a fraud. Also, XXXX ( a few years back ) and now CitiXXXX XXXX  credit companies are eager to process such fraudulent transactions for in-office cash advance coming from an online merchant because they make a profit on it by charging the cash advance fee immediately plus the interest on the purchase amount from day XXXX without typical 30 day grace period for customers paying their full balance on time.
